core_mechanism: a threshold-triggered nonperturbative instability of a ground state, in which an external driving field above a critical strength spontaneously decays into paired excitations (background/vacuum decay via a critical-field threshold)
brief_reason: The abstract centers on vacuum polarization in a strong field and the concept of a critical electric field above which particle-antiparticle (electron-positron) pairs are spontaneously produced, i.e. a critical-threshold instability that converts a background state into paired excitations.
